headline: KTOS Stock Declines 6% in a Month: Opportunity or Warning Sign?
summary: Kratos Defense is expanding across drones, hypersonics and satellite systems, backed by a $14.3B pipeline and new missile-defense awards.

headline: Teledyne (TDY): Buy, Sell, or Hold Post Q1 Earnings?
summary: Teledyne has had an impressive run over the past six months as its shares have beaten the S&P 500 by 8.4%. The stock now trades at $618.31, marking a 19.4% gain. This was partly thanks to its solid quarterly results, and the run-up might have investors contemplating their next move.

headline: OSIS Gains From Robust Demand, Faces Margin and Timing Headwinds
summary: OSI Systems faces a Mexico program step-down, but a record $1.9B backlog and improving cash collections could reshape the next few quarters.

raw.headline: OSIS Gains From Robust Demand, Faces Margin and Timing Headwinds

headline: Teledyne FLIR Defense Awarded $11.2 Million U.S. Army Contract for Advanced CBRN Sensor Drone Kits
summary: THOUSAND OAKS, Calif., June 04, 2026--Teledyne Technologies Incorporated (NYSE:TDY) announced today that Teledyne FLIR Defense, a global leader in intelligent sensing and unmanned systems, has won an $11.2 million contract from the U.S. Army’s Capability Program Executive for Chemical, Biological, Radiological and Nuclear Defense (CPE CBRND) to deliver more than 45 advanced CBRN unmanned aerial system (UAS) kits.

raw.headline: Teledyne FLIR Defense Awarded $11.2 Million U.S. Army Contract for Advanced CBRN Sensor Drone Kits
raw.summary: THOUSAND OAKS, Calif., June 04, 2026--Teledyne Technologies Incorporated (NYSE:TDY) announced today that Teledyne FLIR Defense, a global leader in intelligent sensing and unmanned systems, has won an $11.2 million contract from the U.S. Army’s Capability Program Executive for Chemical, Biological, Radiological and Nuclear Defense (CPE CBRND) to deliver more than 45 advanced CBRN unmanned aerial system (UAS) kits.

headline: Teledyne FLIR Defense Awarded $11.2M Contract By The U.S. Army For Over 45 Advanced Unmanned Aerial System Kits
summary: The kits were developed under the Army's Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear Sensor Integration on Robotic Platforms (CSIRP) program. CSIRP focuses on rapidly prototyping and fielding modular sensor solutions
